workflow.name: Reconciliation Anomaly Resolution
workflow.description:
# UC4: Reconciliation Anomaly Resolution

Manual resolution of reconciliation anomalies discovered after end-of-day reconciliation. Each step is conditional on the anomaly type identified during investigation and requires analyst approval before firing.

## Anomaly Types

**Control orphans** -- per-file Control residuals with no Clearing counterpart:

1. **Resolve Control Orphan** (T5406) -- Flush orphaned Control through Suspense and allocate to a destination account (e.g. Bank Fees) in a single atomic 2-leg event.

**Suspense orphans** -- aged balances remaining after flush, classified by investigation:

2. **Resolve Bank Fee** (T5403) -- DR Bank Fees (50100), CR Suspense (10103). Recognizes the charge as an expense.
3. **Resolve Erroneous Debit** (T5404) -- DR Clearing (10101), CR Suspense (10103). Re-enters normal clearing/flush cycle when the bank posts a correction.
4. **Resolve Unrecognized Credit** (T5405) -- DR Suspense (10103), CR Bank Fees (50100). Recognizes the credit as a fee refund or operational offset.

**Final state:** Suspense returns to zero. Anomaly is classified and resolved on the correct account.

steps.description: Flush an orphaned Control per-file residual (no Clearing counterpart) through Suspense and allocate to destination account (e.g., Bank Fees 50100) in a single atomic 2-leg event. Clears the per-file Control instance and nets Suspense to zero.
steps.condition_description: Fires when a per-file Control instance has a non-zero balance after all matching is complete, meaning one or more bank statement entries had no Clearing counterpart.

steps.description: Resolve an aged Suspense orphan identified as a bank service charge (wire fee, maintenance fee, overdraft charge). Debits Bank Fees expense (50100) and credits Suspense (10103), recognizing the fee as an expense per GAAP.
steps.condition_description: Fires when investigation classifies the aged Suspense orphan as a bank service charge.

steps.description: Reclassify an aged Suspense orphan identified as a bank error by moving the amount from Suspense to Clearing (debit Clearing 10101, credit Suspense 10103). Clearing then carries the expected correction, which re-enters the normal ingestion and flush cycle when the bank posts the correcting entry.
steps.condition_description: Fires when investigation classifies the aged Suspense orphan as a bank erroneous debit pending correction.

steps.description: Resolve an orphaned inbound credit in Suspense (bank reported a credit with no matching Clearing entry). Debits Suspense (10103) and credits Bank Fees (50100) to recognize the credit as a fee refund or operational offset.
steps.condition_description: Fires when investigation classifies the aged Suspense orphan as an unrecognized inbound credit (e.g., fee refund, interest payment, bank operational credit).
